channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kenya Maintains Fuel Stability Through Government-to-Government Import Deal
item.description: Kenya has managed to keep fuel supply and prices relatively stable thanks to a Government-to-Government (G-to-G) import arrangement, even as tensions in the Middle East, particularly around the Strait...

item.title: FCT-IRS Gives Taxpayers Extra Month to Submit Annual Returns
item.description: The Federal Capital Territory Internal Revenue Service (FCT-IRS) don give all individual taxpayers extra one month to fit submit their annual income tax returns. Originally, di deadline na March 31, b...

item.title: Court of Appeal Upholds Death Sentence for Four Convicted of Murder
item.description: Arusha – The Court of Appeal has confirmed the death sentence for four men found guilty of killing Rashid Hassan and throwing him into Kyarano River. The four, John Mbatira, Sadick Shaban, Manga Mgono...

item.title: U.S. Farmers Reduce Wheat Acreage as Profit Margins Shrink
item.description: Across large parts of the United States, farmers are scaling back on wheat production, shifting their focus to more profitable and resilient crops. According to reports from agricultural economists an...

item.title: Kenya High Commission Hosts Nairobi City Thunder Ahead of BAL Kalahari Conference Match
item.description: Pretoria, South Africa – The High Commission of Kenya in Pretoria hosted the Nairobi City Thunder ahead of their critical Basketball Africa League (BAL) Kalahari Conference match against the reigning ...

item.title: BTS set new record with 'ARIRANG' comeback after military service break
item.description: South Korean boy band BTS don return with their new album ‘ARIRANG’ after four years break because of military service, and dem don already break plenty records. The album drop on March 20, 2026, and ...

item.title: Brad Underwood Leads Illinois to First Final Four Appearance Since 2005
item.description: Brad Underwood, now in his 39th year of coaching, has led the University of Illinois men's basketball team to the Final Four for the first time since 2005. The 62-year-old coach spent decades in lower...

item.title: Minister of Power denies resignation amid 2027 election rumours
item.description: The Minister of Power, Chief Adebayo Adelabu, don come out strongly to deny all di rumours wey dey circulate say him don resign from him position. According to him Special Adviser on Media and Publici...

item.title: Cleric from Bihar arrested in UP over controversial remarks about CM Adityanath’s mother
item.description: The Uttar Pradesh Police have taken into custody a cleric from Bihar, Maulana Abdullah Salim, following his alleged objectionable comments regarding the mother of Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath. The r...

item.title: Proposed FOI amendments could significantly limit public access to Ontario government records
item.description: Proposed amendments to Ontario’s Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act (FOI) introduced on March 26 as part of the provincial budget would exclude the offices of Premier Doug Ford, cabi...

item.title: Capital Group MD Urges Shift from Protests to Policy for Better Governance
item.description: At a leadership forum held at the Kenya School of Government, Capital Group Managing Director Symon Bargurei called upon Kenyans to move from mere protests and complaints towards active participation ...

item.title: India accelerates renewable energy drive to reduce oil dependence amid Middle East instability
item.description: As tensions between Israel and Iran spark fears of wider conflict in the Middle East, India’s power sector is focusing on boosting self-reliance to shield the nation from potential energy disruptions....

item.title: Tanzania invites Turkish investors to boost infrastructure, energy, and agriculture development
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has called upon investors from Turkey to take part in key development projects across the infrastructure, energy, and agriculture sectors. The invitation was extended by the...

item.title: Autorickshaw driver arrested for brutally assaulting a 4-year-old after dispute with child’s father in Palghar
item.description: A shocking incident took place in Vasai, Palghar district of Maharashtra, where an autorickshaw driver allegedly attacked a four-year-old boy following a quarrel with the child’s father. According to ...

item.title: Gikomba Market Traders Face Losses as County Clears Riverbank Structures
item.description: Nairobi's Gikomba Market was thrown into chaos after Nairobi County officials demolished part of the shoe section in an overnight operation. The demolition targeted stalls located on riparian land, wh...

item.title: Five Citizen Science Projects Making Real-World Impacts in Australia
item.description: Citizen science allows members of the public to contribute to scientific research, often filling gaps that professionals cannot cover alone. In Australia, several initiatives have demonstrated the rea...

item.title: King Charles set for US visit amid UK-US diplomatic tensions
item.description: King Charles and Queen Camilla are scheduled to make a state visit to the United States in late April. This visit is part of long-term plans to celebrate 250 years since the U.S. gained independence f...

item.title: Capital FM Hosts Forum to Promote Ethical Leadership and Civic Engagement in Kenya
item.description: Capital FM in Nairobi has taken a strong stance on shaping national discourse by hosting its Leadership and Governance Forum at the Kenya School of Government. The Managing Director, Symon Bargurei, s...

item.title: US Army Suspends Aircrew After Apache Helicopters Hover at Kid Rock's Tennessee Home
item.description: The US Army has suspended the aircrew of two AH-64 Apache helicopters from flight duties following an incident where the aircraft hovered near Kid Rock's Nashville-area mansion on March 28, 2026. The ...

item.title: CRDB recognized as leading trade finance bank in East Africa
item.description: CRDB Bank has been recognised as the 'Best Trade Partner Bank East Africa 2026' by the International Finance Corporation (IFC), appreciating its efforts in boosting trade finance across East and Centr...
